an engineer of national reputation"


Can you not give a general idea
of it's location?"


1T would be exceedingly general.
Probably it will run through Moran
Prairie toward Waverly, thence
through the principal towns of the
Palouse country to Moscow, Idaho.
and, ultimately, to a point or points
on the Snake river. The primary
consideration of shortness Of line and
ease of grade from terminus to ter
minus will govern it's location. If
these considerations prevent the
reaching of populous centers by the
main line, branch lines will be con-
structed. ! can only say that it is our
intention to thoroughly cover the Pa
louise country with our system.


I'VE will enter Spokane over the
lines of the Spokane Traction COM
pany. The Coeur d'Alene & Spokane
Railway company and the Spokane
Traction company now control site
155x175 feet, bounded by Washington
street and Sprague and First avenues.
y terminal company will be formed
to construct an up-to-date depot and
Office building there, and this will be
used for passenger depot and office
purposes by the three companies. Ex-
tensive freight terminals will have
to be provided, but the site for these
has not vet been selected"


"Where will you obtain your cleo
trio power?"


AT some near time we will, no
doubt, find 1t necessary to acquire
n power plant of our own. At the first
we can probably secure sufficient
power from the Washington Water
Power company"


"Mr. Graves. is this going to be
n local enterprise or foreign enter
prise? In other words, will its stock
be owned and its policies dictated by
people of this community, or will it
be controlled by some transconti-
mental railroad or allied interests"


IF our plans are carried out, 1t
will be purely and unquali1edly n
local enterprise As you will observe,
the corporators of this company are
men whose interests lie in Spokane
and the Palouse country. There will
be associated with them in this enter
businesses with which they are con-
